title: Admin Dashboard: Student Registry, Edit Details, Per-Student Sessions & Materials

requirements.summary: Display all registered students in admin Students page with full details including name, email, contact info, enrolled course, and enrollment/payment status
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Students page fetches and displays all students from the backend via React Query
Each student row/card shows name, email, contact info, enrolled course name, and enrollment/payment status
Page renders an empty state message when no students are registered
Search and filter controls work against the displayed student list

file_operations.description: Update the Students page to fetch all registered students using the useGetAllStudents hook from useQueries.ts. Display each student's full details in a table or card layout, showing fullName, email, phone, course, sessionType, hours, enrollmentDate, paymentStatus (with StatusBadge), and isActive flag. Add an empty state message when the students array is empty. Ensure search and filter controls work against the fetched student list.

requirements.summary: Add Edit Student capability in admin Students page to edit all student fields with backend persistence
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Edit' action is available per student in the admin Students page
Clicking Edit opens a modal or inline form pre-populated with the student's current data
Form includes fields for name, email, contact info, enrollment status, and all other student fields stored in the backend
Saving the form calls the backend to persist the changes
The student list refreshes to reflect the updated data after a successful save
Validation prevents saving with empty required fields

file_operations.description: Add a new mutation hook useUpdateStudent that wraps the backend updateStudent function. Accept parameters matching the Student interface fields (principal, fullName, email, phone, course, sessionType, hours, paymentStatus, transactionId, enrollmentDate, isActive). On success, invalidate the 'students' query key to trigger a refetch of the student list.

Add an Edit button or action per student row/card that opens a modal or inline form. Create a form component pre-populated with the selected student's current data, including fields for fullName, email, phone, course, sessionType, hours, paymentStatus (as a select dropdown for UPI/Stripe status), transactionId, enrollmentDate (as a date picker), and isActive (as a checkbox). Use the useUpdateStudent mutation hook to save changes. Add validation to prevent submission with empty required fields (fullName, email, phone, course). Show a success toast on save and close the form. The student list will automatically refresh due to query invalidation.

requirements.summary: Update admin ManageStudentSessions page to allow per-student session assignment using a student selector dropdown
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can select any registered student from a dropdown populated from the backend
Session form includes fields for date, time, Google Meet link, and topic
On submission, the session is saved and associated only with the selected student
The session appears in the selected student's sessions list on their student dashboard
The session does NOT appear on other students' dashboards
Admin can view and delete sessions per selected student
Sessions list updates immediately after adding or deleting

file_operations.description: Update the ManageStudentSessions page to add a student selector dropdown at the top. Populate the dropdown with all registered students fetched via useGetAllStudents, displaying each student's fullName and email. When a student is selected, store their Principal in component state. Update the session form to pass the selected studentPrincipal to the addSession mutation from useSessions.ts (which already accepts studentPrincipal as the first parameter). Display the sessions list for the selected student using the existing useGetSessionsForStudent hook with the selected Principal. Ensure the delete session functionality works per selected student. Add empty state messages when no student is selected or when the selected student has no sessions.

requirements.summary: Update admin ManageStudentMaterials page to allow per-student material assignment using a student selector dropdown
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can select any registered student from a dropdown populated from the backend
Materials form includes fields for title, description, and a file/URL link
On submission, the material is saved and associated only with the selected student
The material appears in the selected student's materials page on their student dashboard
The material does NOT appear on other students' dashboards
Admin can view and delete materials per selected student
Materials list updates immediately after adding or deleting

file_operations.description: Update the ManageStudentMaterials page to add a student selector dropdown at the top. Populate the dropdown with all registered students fetched via useGetAllStudents, displaying each student's fullName and email. When a student is selected, store their Principal in component state. Update the materials form to pass the selected studentPrincipal to the addMaterial mutation from useMaterials.ts (which already accepts studentPrincipal as the first parameter). The form should include fields for title, description (optional), fileData (optional file upload converted to Uint8Array), fileLink (optional URL string), and relatedCourse. Display the materials list for the selected student using the existing useGetMaterialsForStudent hook with the selected Principal. Ensure the delete material functionality works per selected student. Add empty state messages when no student is selected or when the selected student has no materials.

requirements.summary: Ensure admin Dashboard overview page correctly displays recent students list and summary stats from backend
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Dashboard stat cards show accurate counts fetched from the backend
Recent students section lists students with their enrolled course and status
Data is sourced from backend via React Query, not localStorage

file_operations.description: Verify that the Dashboard page uses the useGetAllStudents hook from useQueries.ts to fetch all students from the backend. Compute the summary stats (total students, active students, pending payments, approved payments) by processing the fetched students array. Display StatCard components with these computed counts. In the recent students section, map over the students array (sorted by enrollmentDate descending, limited to 5-10 recent entries) and display each student's fullName, course, paymentStatus (with StatusBadge), and enrollmentDate. Remove any references to localStorage for student data. Add loading states and error handling for the query.
